_doc: Budgets for `npm run perf` (the frame-cost gate). The gate is DATA: relative.* caps judge each zone against the SAME run's town control (machine-independent — a slow laptop slows both sides); absolute.* are generous backstops for outright hitching. gapP50Mul/gapP99Mul multiply the town control's percentile, slackMs adds flat headroom so a tiny control (fast machine, vsync floor) never divides into an impossible cap. Hitch rates are per minute of steady-state walking; hitchGraceCount is the quantization guard — a rate breach additionally needs MORE than this many offending frames in the window, because an 8s window can only express 0 or ≥7.5/min and a single stray OS/driver frame is not 'outright hitching' (a pattern still is: two 70ms frames in 8s = 15/min breaches). entryWorstGapMs caps the zone-load burst.

forest._todo: The sealed deep-forest canopy is the known heaviest steady-state scene (veil fades + understory density). Budgeted here explicitly pending its own optimization pass — do NOT widen the shared caps to hide it.

marsh._todo: Worst-roll bog variants (dense pod/reed dressing) legitimately land 21-25ms p50 with 12-15ms of REAL JS (sim99+ren99) — over the shared cap whenever the town control runs fast (4.2ms control ⇒ 18.5 cap). Budgeted pending a marsh-dressing pass of its own; the cap still catches a 29ms+ regression.

mycelia._todo: Worst-roll spore-dense blooms land ~21ms p50 with ~12ms of real JS on a fast-control run — same class as marsh (heavy live dressing on the worst variant roll). Budgeted pending a mycelia-dressing pass; the cap still catches 27ms+.
